skill.name: discharge-summary-writer
skill.description: Generate structured hospital discharge summaries from patient admission data, hospital course, medications, and follow-up instructions with bilingual Chinese-English output.
skill.summary: Generate hospital discharge summaries from admission data, hospital course.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 83 patterns across 7 files, triggering a high-risk score (100/100). After evaluation, all 83 findings are false positives from pattern-based scanning: markdown backticks flagged as shell execution, 'MD'/'DES' substrings flagged as weak cryptography, Chinese medical text flagged as high-entropy payloads, and security checklist documentation (../) flagged as path traversal. One legitimate low-severity concern exists: the Python script writes output files to user-specified paths without path traversal validation, which is documented expected behavior for a CLI tool. The skill includes explicit safety warnings requiring physician review, a security checklist, and clear scope boundaries. No malware, obfuscation, network exfiltration, or prompt injection detected.

low_findings.title: File write to user-controlled output path
low_findings.description: The Python script at scripts/main.py:312 writes generated content to a user-specified --output path without path traversal validation. Path('../../outside.txt') would succeed, allowing writes outside the intended workspace. This is inherent CLI tool behavior but should be documented as a risk for users running the script in shared environments.

dangerous_patterns.title: False positive: Markdown backticks detected as shell execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 44 occurrences of markdown inline code backticks and code fence blocks (```) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All instances are standard markdown formatting in SKILL.md and references/section_guidelines.md. No actual shell command execution via backticks exists in any file.

dangerous_patterns.title: False positive: Substring matches detected as weak cryptographic algorithms
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 27 occurrences of 'weak cryptographic algorithm' triggered by substrings: 'MD' within markdown headers (##) and 'DES' within words like 'description' and 'discharge'. No actual cryptographic code or weak algorithm usage exists anywhere in the skill files.

dangerous_patterns.title: False positive: Security checklist text detected as path traversal
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)' at SKILL.md lines 222 and 225 as path traversal sequences. These are documentation entries in the skill's security checklist describing protections, not actual traversal attempts.

dangerous_patterns.title: False positive: Chinese medical text detected as high-entropy payload
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 7 instances of high-entropy strings in references/example_patient.json as potential encoded/encrypted payloads. Chinese characters (UTF-8) naturally produce higher byte entropy than ASCII English. All flagged content is legitimate sample patient data with realistic names, medical history, and clinical details.

dangerous_patterns.title: False positive: GitHub source URL detected as hardcoded URL and network risk
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged the markdown blockquote attribution link at SKILL.md:7 as both a hardcoded URL and network risk. This is a standard GitHub source attribution in documentation, not executable code and not a network call.

dangerous_patterns.title: False positive: Medical guideline text detected as system/network reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged 18 occurrences of system and network reconnaissance across SKILL.md, references/discharge_template.md, and references/section_guidelines.md. All flagged lines contain medical writing guidelines, patient care instructions, or markdown formatting. No system reconnaissance commands (uname, whoami, hostname) or network scanning tools exist in any file.

content.user_title: Generate Clinical Discharge Summaries from Patient Data
content.value_statement: Medical professionals spend hours writing discharge summaries from unstructured patient records. This skill automates summary generation from structured patient data, producing bilingual Chinese-English discharge documents that follow clinical standards while requiring mandatory physician review.
actual_capabilities: Generates complete discharge summaries from structured JSON patient data input
Produces bilingual Chinese-English output with standardized medical terminology
Supports multiple output formats: standard markdown, structured sections, and machine-readable JSON
Validates required patient data fields before generation and reports missing information
Calculates hospital stay duration automatically from admission and discharge dates
Includes built-in physician disclaimer and signature block in every generated document
limitations: Does not connect to live Electronic Medical Record (EMR) systems; requires manual data input
Cannot validate medication interactions, dosages, or cross-reference drug allergies
Does not generate or verify ICD-10 diagnosis codes automatically
Output requires mandatory physician review before clinical use; never use generated summaries without verification

use_cases.title: Hospital Discharge Workflow
use_cases.description: A resident physician enters structured patient data after a hospital stay and receives a complete discharge summary draft with all required sections filled in, ready for attending physician review and signature.

use_cases.title: Medical Documentation Training
use_cases.description: Medical students and trainees use the skill to learn standard discharge summary structure by comparing their manual drafts against generated templates that follow clinical documentation guidelines.

use_cases.title: Cross-Language Medical Reporting
use_cases.description: Clinicians in bilingual healthcare settings generate discharge summaries with side-by-side Chinese-English content, ensuring both patients and international care teams can understand the documentation.
use_cases.target_user: Bilingual clinicians and international medical teams

prompt_templates.title: Basic Discharge Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a discharge summary for a patient with the following details: 68-year-old male, admitted on 2025-01-28 with chest pain and tightness for 3 days, diagnosed with acute anterior wall STEMI. Underwent coronary angiography and stent placement on admission day. Hospital course was uncomplicated. Discharged on 2025-02-05 in improved condition. Include discharge medications: aspirin 100mg daily, clopidogrel 75mg daily, atorvastatin 20mg nightly, metoprolol 47.5mg daily. Follow-up in cardiology clinic in 1 week.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple single-diagnosis admission with uncomplicated hospital course

prompt_templates.title: Complex Multi-Diagnosis Case
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive discharge summary for a patient with multiple comorbidities. Include: admission for acute exacerbation of COPD with secondary pneumonia, past medical history of hypertension (10 years), type 2 diabetes (5 years), and prior appendectomy. Hospital course included IV antibiotics for 5 days, respiratory therapy, and diabetic management adjustments. Note that the patient developed a mild antibiotic-related rash on day 3 which resolved after switching medications. Discharge diagnoses should prioritize the primary respiratory condition followed by chronic conditions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex admission with comorbidities, complications, and medication changes

prompt_templates.title: Surgical Admission Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a discharge summary for a surgical patient: 45-year-old female admitted for elective laparoscopic cholecystectomy on 2025-03-10. Pre-operative diagnosis: symptomatic cholelithiasis confirmed by ultrasound. Surgery performed on 2025-03-11 without complications. Post-operative course: pain well-controlled, tolerated clear liquids on post-op day 1, advanced to regular diet on day 2. Discharged on 2025-03-13. Include wound care instructions for laparoscopic incision sites and activity restrictions for 2 weeks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Surgical admission with pre-operative, intra-operative, and post-operative documentation

prompt_templates.title: Patient with Complications and Consultations
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a discharge summary for a 72-year-old male admitted with community-acquired pneumonia who developed acute kidney injury (AKI) during hospitalization. Include: admission details with CURB-65 score, initial antibiotic regimen, nephrology consultation on day 3 with recommendations for medication dose adjustment, cardiology consultation for new-onset atrial fibrillation found on telemetry. AKI resolved with IV fluids and medication adjustment. Document all consultant recommendations, the resolution of each complication, and specific follow-up plans with both nephrology and cardiology.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced case with multiple consultations, complications, and specialty follow-up coordination

output_examples.input: Patient data JSON for a 68-year-old male with acute anterior wall STEMI, hypertension, and type 2 diabetes, admitted Jan 28 and discharged Feb 5 after successful PCI
output: ## Basic patient information / 患者基本信息
- 冠心病 急性前壁ST段抬高型心肌梗死
## Inpatient diagnosis and treatment process / 住院诊疗经过
患者入院后立即启动胸痛中心绿色通道...于入院后2小时行急诊冠脉造影，示前降支近段95%狭窄，植入药物洗脱支架1枚...
- 冠心病 急性前壁ST段抬高型心肌梗死
### Condition at discharge / 出院时状况
## Discharge with medicine / 出院带药
1. **阿司匹林肠溶片** | 剂量: 100mg | 频率: 每日一次
2. **硫酸氢氯吡格雷片** | 剂量: 75mg | 频率: 每日一次
- 心内科门诊：出院后1周（2025-02-12），周二上午
- 胸痛、胸闷，尤其是持续不缓解超过15分钟

output_examples.input: Structured format request for the same patient data
output: Demographics table with name, age, gender, MRN, admission/discharge dates, department, attending physician
3.3 Significant Findings / 重要检查结果

best_practices: Always verify all input data against the official medical record before generating a summary; never fabricate or assume clinical details
Include explicit assumptions in the output when any patient data field is incomplete or ambiguous
Require a licensed physician to review, correct, and sign every generated discharge summary before it enters the medical record
anti_patterns: Do not use generated summaries as final medical documents without physician review and signature; this creates legal liability and patient safety risks
Do not input real patient data into public or shared AI environments without appropriate HIPAA-compliant safeguards and data use agreements
Do not rely on the skill to catch clinical contradictions, medication errors, or missing diagnoses; it assembles provided data without clinical reasoning

faq.question: Does this skill connect to hospital EMR systems?
faq.answer: No. This skill requires manually prepared JSON input. It cannot access live electronic medical record systems, hospital databases, or any network resources.

faq.question: Is the generated discharge summary ready for clinical use?
faq.answer: No. Every generated summary includes a disclaimer and requires review, correction, and signature by a licensed attending physician before it can be used as a medical document.

faq.question: What languages does the skill support?
faq.answer: The skill generates bilingual Chinese-English output by default. Every section heading includes both languages. The content language can be set to Chinese (zh) or English (en) using the --language parameter.

faq.question: What input data format does the skill require?
faq.answer: The skill requires a JSON file with six sections: patient_info, admission_data, hospital_course, discharge_status, medications, and follow_up. Each section has specific required fields documented in the SKILL.md file.

faq.question: Can the skill validate medication dosages or interactions?
faq.answer: No. The skill assembles medication information from provided data but does not perform clinical validation of dosages, drug interactions, allergy cross-referencing, or therapeutic appropriateness. A pharmacist or physician must verify all medications.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: Three formats are supported: standard (human-readable bilingual markdown), structured (sectioned markdown for EMR integration), and JSON (machine-readable data for system-to-system transfer).
